message:
feat(storage): tick 176 shutdown enqueue routes through send_with_cx (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Follow-on to tick 175 (b776928b, d7a1ace2): `shutdown_with_cx` was the
last remaining storage-write method whose mpsc enqueue still used the
plain `send(…)` call instead of cx-aware `send_with_cx(cx, …)`. With
this change, every write-path enqueue in StorageHandle now observes cx.

Why this matters beyond consistency: a cx-cancelled shutdown caller
previously held the mpsc reserve until backpressure naturally drained,
which is visible when shutdown races a saturating writer queue (the
scenario the doc comment already called out for the writer_handle-take
skip path). Routing through send_with_cx means the reserve is released
immediately on cancel, unblocking other callers waiting on queue slots.

No behavior change on the happy path: same WriteCommand::Shutdown
variant, same recv_writer_shutdown_ack wait, same feature gate. Only
the enqueue primitive changes.

Closes the tick-175/176 "orphan cx holes in storage writes" cleanup.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
